kept.text:
The eggshell-in-the-garden thing is folklore.

Crushed eggshells don't deter slugs. Researchers have actually tested this. Slugs crawl over them, around them, through them. 

The "sharp edges cut their bellies" claim is something pinterest said once and the internet decided to believe forever.

Eggshells also don't add calcium to your soil at any meaningful rate. The shell is calcium carbonate, which has to break down before plants can absorb it, and that process takes years. 

Your tomatoes won't see any of it this season. Or next. Or the one after that most likely.

If you want to deter slugs, the best thing you can do is pick them off your plants by hand at dusk. 

If you want to add calcium, get a soil test first to confirm you actually need to, then use gypsum or lime.

Eggshells in the compost are fine. Eggshells around your seedlings are decoration.
